speaker: Brian Holman (guest lecturer; consulting engineer and former colleague of Prof. Eagar)
speaker_notes: Lecture delivered entirely by Brian Holman in Eagar's absence. Eagar is referenced throughout in the third person; captioner garbles the surname as Eager/Iger/Egret/Igar — all normalized to [Eagar] in layer 2 per convention 9.
topic_summary: Material substitution case studies: refrigerator shelving (metal to plastic), food/beverage containers (glass to polymer), water and gas conveyance (clay to concrete to wood to cast iron to HDPE), domestic plumbing (lead-tin solder to swage), CSST gas tubing and lightning-puncture fires, lithium-ion battery thermal runaway, IGU glass curtain wall failures. Second half: testing and inspection methods (ultrasonic, tensile, Charpy impact, metallurgical mounts, CT crack-growth, magnetic particle, eddy current); rail fatigue and Sperry car inspection; full-scale wire rope test; Holman's master's-thesis work on aluminum foam sandwich structures for Navy topside.
